title: Existence and Uniqueness Theorem & Picard's Iteration Method
description: Introduction to the Existence and Uniqueness Theorem

frontMatter.title: Existence and Uniqueness Theorem & Picard's Iteration Method
frontMatter.topic: Existence and Uniqueness of Solutions, Picard's Iteration
frontMatter.summary: Covers the Existence and Uniqueness Theorem for IVPs and introduces Picard's Iteration Method for finding solutions.
table_of_contents: Introduction to the Existence and Uniqueness Theorem
The Picard-Lindelöf Existence and Uniqueness Theorem
Equivalence of IVP and Integral Equation

key_formulas.name: Initial Value Problem
key_formulas.note: Standard form of a first-order IVP.

key_formulas.name: Translated IVP
key_formulas.note: IVP translated to the origin for simplified analysis.

key_formulas.name: Existence Interval Alpha
key_formulas.note: Defines the interval $|x| \le \alpha$ for which a unique solution exists.

key_formulas.name: Integral Equation
key_formulas.note: Equivalent form of the IVP, fundamental for Picard's method.

key_formulas.name: Picard's Iteration Formula
key_formulas.note: Generates a sequence of approximations to the solution.

key_formulas.name: Lipschitz Condition
key_formulas.note: Required for the uniqueness part of Picard-Lindelöf theorem.
